text.title: Go Project Structure Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Go Project Structure application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: `cmd/<name>/main.go` files contain only argument parsing, dependency wiring, and `app.Run(ctx)`. Business logic lives in internal/.
rule.rationale: Logic in cmd/ is impossible to test in isolation and cannot be reused across multiple binaries; pushing it down to internal/ keeps cmd/ minimal.

rule.statement: Application code lives under internal/. The Go toolchain enforces internal/ as private to the module; external consumers cannot import it.
rule.rationale: internal/ is the only language-enforced boundary; using it gets you private-by-default without import linters.

rule.statement: pkg/<name> exists only when an external consumer (other modules) will import the package. Internal-only code never lives in pkg/.
rule.rationale: Many projects start with pkg/everything because tutorials show it; the result is no real boundary. pkg/ is meaningful only when public API is the intent.

rule.statement: Imports flow inward: cmd → internal/<feature> → internal/shared. Cross-feature dependencies go through interfaces declared at the consumer side. No cycles.
rule.rationale: Cycles are a build error in Go; preventing them by convention preserves the option to extract modules later.

rule.statement: A single Makefile (or Justfile/Mage equivalent) provides build, test, lint, run, docker targets. Ad-hoc bash scripts at top level are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Loose scripts accumulate; a single orchestration point is auditable + reproducible across machines.

rule.statement: Top-level contains only: go.mod, go.sum, Makefile, README.md, .gitignore, .golangci.yml, Dockerfile, .github/. No loose .go files, no scratch dirs.
rule.rationale: Top-level clutter signals architecture rot; keeping it minimal forces decisions about where new code belongs.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
